claim: GenAI agent development does not primarily belong to data scientists or ML engineers because Anthropic/OpenAI already ran the data pipeline; what remains is prompt/context engineering, distributed systems, and functional evaluation
actionable: Assemble diverse teams for agent development: product engineers, systems engineers, domain experts, non-technical subject matter experts, not just data scientists
source_title: Does GenAI belong to data scientists?

claim: Data scientists add value through guardrails: LLM knowledge, risk understanding from stats background, LLM-as-judge evaluation (beyond simple precision/recall), and fine-tuning if needed
actionable: Include data scientists in agent teams for eval pipeline design, LLM-as-judge validation, and risk assessment rather than model training
source_title: Does GenAI belong to data scientists?

claim: Data scientists often obsess over traditional ML metrics (precision, recall, F1) which don't measure agent functional performance; need broader evaluation surface covering actual business outcomes
actionable: Design agent evals around functional performance, business outcomes, and user satisfaction, not traditional ML metrics; include domain experts in evaluation
source_title: Does GenAI belong to data scientists?

claim: Ideal team: product/systems engineers implement requirements; domain experts do human annotation and prompt engineering (closest to problem); data scientists design eval pipelines
actionable: Form cross-functional teams where domain experts control prompts, engineers build systems, and data scientists build eval infrastructure
source_title: Does GenAI belong to data scientists?

claim: LLMs are just APIs; product engineers familiar with API integration can build agents; distributed agent systems with multiple sub-agents is more of a systems engineering problem than ML
actionable: Hire systems and product engineers for agent architecture; ML expertise is less critical than systems thinking for multi-agent deployments
source_title: Does GenAI belong to data scientists?
